critique.defects: Using == (loose comparison) instead of === for post type check allows type coercion attacks; $_GET['post'] is not cast to int before passing to get_post_type(), risking NULL pointer or unexpected behavior; no nonce verification on any $_GET access even though phpcs comments say verified elsewhere

quality_improvements: Changed == to === for strict comparison on post_type
Added is_numeric check before calling get_post_type()
Cast $_GET['post'] to int before use

critique.defects: $_GET['_locale'] and $_GET['wp_lang'] are read without any nonce verification or request origin validation, enabling potential locale-sniffing CSRF attacks; sanitize_text_field on 'wp_lang' does not validate against a whitelist of supported locales, allowing arbitrary strings through; empty() on $_GET values permits '0' and empty string to pass, creating logic bugs

quality_improvements: Added wp_unslash() for proper superglobal sanitization
Added whitelist validation against wp_available_languages()
Changed isset/empty check to prevent empty-string edge cases

critique.defects: Using == for $loop['i'] == -1 comparison instead of === allows type coercion; no input validation on $loop parameter — caller can pass non-array types and cause wp_parse_args to behave unexpectedly; no sanitization of any $_POST/$_GET data that feeds into loop values, risking XSS when those values are rendered

quality_improvements: Changed == to === for strict integer comparison

critique.defects: No nonce verification on the generated nonce — the nonce is created but never validated on form submission, making file upload CSRF-protected only if the caller verifies it; $attrs['name'] is used in explode() without isset() guard, causing PHP notices when 'name' key is missing; $field_key extracted from string manipulation has no length/type validation, risking oversized nonce generation

quality_improvements: Added isset() and is_string() guard on $attrs['name']
Added strlen() guard on $field_key to prevent oversized nonce keys
Added esc_attr() on $field_key in output to prevent XSS in nonce field name

critique.defects: meta_key LIKE '%field_%' uses unescaped wildcard — the '_' in 'field_%' is interpreted as a single-character SQL wildcard, causing it to match unintended meta_keys; $ofg->ID is not verified as an object or property, risking null dereference; no check that $field has a 'key' offset after unserialization, causing undefined index warnings if unserialize returns unexpected types

quality_improvements: Escaped LIKE wildcards '_' to literal underscore
Added $ofg object validation before accessing ->ID
Added isset/is_array checks on $field after unserialization
Added $nfg array guard before accessing ['ID']

critique.defects: SQL injection risk: $taxonomy is interpolated directly into LIKE pattern before prepare() — if $taxonomy contains '%', it matches too broadly; the double str_replace for escaping '_' is incorrect for SQL (should use '\_') and str_replace runs on already-escaped string causing double-escaping; $row['option_value'] used without null check in insert(); term_id from preg matches is used directly in $wpdb->insert() without intval casting, risking string injection

quality_improvements: Added sanitize_key() on $taxonomy input
Used addcslashes() instead of str_replace() for proper SQL escaping
Cast $term_id to int before DB insert
Added isset() guard on $row['option_value']

critique.defects: $_POST['query'] accessed without isset() on intermediate keys — $_POST['query']['_acfuploader'] triggers PHP undefined index notice if $_POST['query'] doesn't exist; $_POST values are not passed through wp_unslash(), violating WordPress sanitization standards; no nonce check on $_POST access, allowing any caller to trigger field lookups

quality_improvements: Added wp_unslash() on all $_POST values per WordPress standards
Initialized $field_key to false for cleaner isset() check

critique.defects: No capability check (current_user_can) on AJAX function — any logged-in user can invoke this; no wp_verify_nonce() to prevent CSRF on AJAX requests; relies entirely on get_source_field() which itself lacks nonce verification; wp_ajax_ functions should also call wp_die() after wp_send_json to prevent output leakage

quality_improvements: Added check_ajax_referer() for CSRF protection
Added capability check current_user_can('edit_posts')

critique.defects: $_POST['acf'] is directly assigned from $values without any validation — caller can overwrite $_POST['acf'] with arbitrary data; no nonce verification on the save operation; wp_kses_post_deep on nested arrays may not properly sanitize deeply nested objects; $post_id is used without sanitization (should be intval)

quality_improvements: Added is_array() check on $values before assignment to $_POST
Cast $post_id to int for safety

critique.defects: No nonce verification — this function processes POST data without any CSRF protection; $_POST['acf'] used without wp_unslash(); no capability check; empty() check allows false/0 values to pass, creating potential data corruption when post_id is 0

quality_improvements: Added current_user_can capability check
Changed empty() to isset()/is_array() for proper validation
Added wp_unslash() on $_POST['acf']
Cast $post_id to int

critique.defects: Using != instead of !== for $preview_id != $post_id comparison allows type coercion; $_GET['p'] and $_GET['page_id'] cast to int before isset() check — should check isset first; $revision->post_parent accessed without null check on $revision object; cache key $k uses raw $post_id which may not be string-int compatible

quality_improvements: Changed != to !== for strict comparison
Added wp_unslash() on $_GET values
Added is_object() check on $revision
Cast $k to string for cache key safety

critique.defects: $sitepress global assumed to exist — no null check before calling methods, causing fatal error if WPML is not installed; no check for function_exists('icl_get_languages') or class_exists('WPML') before accessing global; constructors should not have side effects like add_action/add_filter at top level without guards

quality_improvements: Added is_object() and method_exists() guards on $sitepress
Early return if WPML is not available

critique.defects: $_REQUEST['lang'] used without any validation against supported languages — allows language switching for any arbitrary string, potentially causing WPML to fail or switch to an unintended locale; no check that $sitepress object exists before calling switch_lang(); $_REQUEST (instead of $_GET/$_POST) broadens attack surface; no nonce verification on the language switch

quality_improvements: Added $sitepress object/method existence checks
Added wp_unslash() on $_REQUEST value
Added whitelist validation against supported languages

critique.defects: $_POST['_acf_screen'] cast to string without wp_unslash(); $name concatenated into output string without sanitization, potential XSS if $name comes from user input; strlen() on $input_prefix with no strlen check — if $input_prefix is empty, substr returns false causing warning

quality_improvements: Added wp_unslash() and sanitize_text_field() on $_POST['_acf_screen']
Added sanitize_key() on $post_type
Added empty-string guard before strlen() call
Added esc_html() on output to prevent XSS

critique.defects: $_REQUEST[$k] used without wp_unslash() before acf_sanitize_request_args; no whitelist or validation on $k — caller can specify any request key name, enabling request smuggling attacks; array input $args not validated, allowing non-array types to cause foreach to fail

quality_improvements: Added is_array() validation on $args
Added wp_unslash() on $_REQUEST values

critique.defects: wp_ajax_nopriv hook allows unauthenticated users to trigger validation AJAX — this may bypass authentication checks; no validation that WordPress and ACF core are loaded before registering hooks; the nopriv action combined with nonce verification failure in ajax_validate_save_post still results in a JSON response being sent, which may leak information

quality_improvements: Removed wp_ajax_nopriv hook to prevent unauthenticated AJAX access
Added function_exists() gate for WordPress bootstrap check

critique.defects: acf_verify_ajax() is called but its implementation may not include nonce verification (depends on implementation); $nonce_error strings constructed without esc_html__(), potentially allowing XSS if __() is filtered; wp_send_json_success() does not set proper HTTP status code for errors (returns 200); no check_ajax_referer() before processing — nonce_error path sends useful info to attackers about nonce mechanism

quality_improvements: Added direct wp_verify_nonce() check with generic error message
Added esc_html__() on all translatable strings
Added esc_url_raw() on external URL
Changed wp_send_json_success to wp_send_json_error for failure responses

critique.defects: $_POST['acf'] used without wp_unslash() before passing to acf_validate_values; no nonce verification in this validation path; $screen and $post_type from acf_request_arg pass through in_array whitelist but no explicit input sanitization on the values themselves; no capability check before validation

quality_improvements: Added wp_unslash() on $_POST['acf'] before validation

critique.defects: $data['term'] is stored as-is from is_numeric() check — could be a float like '123.45' causing issues downstream; get_term_by('slug', ...) with a numeric string term is an anti-pattern (should use get_term by ID); $data['taxonomy'] from $wpdb->get_var() not checked for null before passing to get_term_by(); explode without limit on user input could create large arrays

quality_improvements: Added floor() check to ensure $value is integer, cast to (int)
Added explode limit of 2 to prevent array bloat
Added sanitize_key() on taxonomy and sanitize_text_field on term
Added null check on $wpdb->get_var result
Added is_wp_error() check on get_term result

critique.defects: $_POST['action'] accessed via acf_maybe_get without wp_unslash(); no validation that $action parameter is a string, allowing unexpected types in comparison

quality_improvements: Added is_string() check on $action parameter for safe comparison
